description: First-contact source-audit flow using secknowledge-skill and hack-skills as bounded methodology sources.

workflow.description: Security-knowledge one-run baseline: classify the target, model threats, run focused audit tracks, validate candidates, and write a final report.

agents.description: Classifies target attack surface and selects bounded security-knowledge tracks.
agents.mission: Read repository inventory, module map, and architecture evidence. Classify target type, exposed interfaces, authentication model, AI/agent surfaces, deployment indicators, and likely security-knowledge tracks. Use external skills only as routing methodology; do not claim vulnerabilities.

agents.description: Audits Web/API authentication, authorization, identity, and business-logic controls.
agents.mission: Audit route/API/controller/RPC surfaces for authentication bypass, broken object/function authorization, JWT/OAuth/OIDC flaws, session issues, privilege boundaries, business-logic abuse, and multi-tenant isolation. Produce only source-evidenced candidate findings.

agents.description: Audits injection, deserialization, template, XML, and code-execution sinks.
agents.mission: Trace attacker-controlled input into SQL/NoSQL, command, expression, template, JNDI, deserialization, XML/XXE, XSS, and code execution sinks. Require source-to-sink reachability and missing or bypassable guards before reporting candidates.

agents.description: Audits file, upload, SSRF, protocol, browser boundary, and request parsing risks.
agents.mission: Audit file upload/download, archive extraction, path construction, URL fetchers, SSRF filters, protocol handling, request smuggling, host header trust, CORS/CSRF, and WebSocket boundaries using local source evidence only.

agents.description: Audits AI, LLM, RAG, MCP, agent, and sandbox security surfaces.
agents.mission: Audit LLM prompt injection, indirect prompt injection, RAG/data poisoning, agent tool overreach, MCP configuration trust, memory/context leakage, model/data handling, and sandbox or container isolation risks. Separate source evidence from deployment assumptions.

agents.description: Audits source management, dependency confusion, deployment, container, and exposure risks.
agents.mission: Audit repository exposure, dependency and package namespace trust, build/release scripts, container/Kubernetes deployment indicators, unauthenticated services, subdomain takeover leads, WAF bypass-sensitive assumptions, and hardening gaps using local evidence.

agents.description: Validates and deduplicates security-knowledge candidate findings.
agents.mission: Re-read cited source paths, challenge attacker control, reachability, missing controls, impact, and authorization assumptions. Treat payloads and external references as methodology, not proof. Classify findings as confirmed, likely, needs-info, or false-positive.

agents.description: Writes the final security-knowledge audit report from validated evidence.
agents.mission: Synthesize classification, threat model, validated findings, rejected candidates, coverage gaps, and referenced methodology into a final report. Only confirmed and likely findings may be reported as vulnerabilities.

stages.objective: Classify target security domains and bind each selected audit track to source evidence and available skills.
Turn classification into source-grounded threat hypotheses and a bounded audit track plan.
Audit Web/API authentication, authorization, identity, and business logic using source evidence only.
Audit injection and deserialization classes with source-to-sink evidence.
Audit file, SSRF, protocol, request parsing, and browser boundary risks.
Audit AI, LLM, agent, MCP, RAG, and sandbox surfaces when present; otherwise document non-applicability with evidence.
Audit source management, dependency confusion, build/release, deployment, and exposure risks using local evidence.
Validate and deduplicate every candidate finding from the security-knowledge audit tracks.
Produce the first-contact security-knowledge audit report from validated evidence.
